evals.prompt: I want an interactive scatter of temperature vs. yield from my DataFrame with an OLS trendline and custom hover tooltips, exported as a standalone HTML file I can put on the lab website.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-plotly: uses plotly.express (px.scatter with trendline='ols'), customizes the hovertemplate, and exports with fig.write_html(...), optionally include_plotlyjs='cdn' to shrink the file.

evals.prompt: Build me a 2x2 interactive dashboard panel — a scatter, a bar chart, a histogram, and a box plot — with subplot titles, so I can pan and zoom each one.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-plotly: uses make_subplots(rows=2, cols=2, ...) with per-cell specs/types, adds go.Scatter/go.Bar/go.Histogram/go.Box traces, and keeps the native interactivity (pan/zoom).

evals.prompt: I have multi-year measurement data. Make an animated scatter that steps through each year with an animation frame, plus a range slider on the time axis for exploration.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-plotly: builds px.scatter(..., animation_frame='year') and enables the rangeslider via fig.update_xaxes(rangeslider_visible=True), leaning on plotly's animation and interactivity features.

evals.prompt: Render my z-grid as a rotatable interactive 3D surface plot I can spin around to inspect the topology in the browser.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-plotly: uses graph objects go.Surface(z=..., x=..., y=...) inside go.Figure to produce an interactive, rotatable 3D surface.

evals.prompt: I need a static, print-ready PDF figure of this line plot for journal submission — vector output, 300 DPI fallback PNG, no interactivity needed.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT invoke alterlab-plotly. A static publication figure with vector PDF output is matplotlib's (alterlab-matplotlib) territory; defers to matplotlib since no interactivity is required.

evals.prompt: Just give me a quick static correlation heatmap of my DataFrame columns with annotated cells for a slide — nothing interactive.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT invoke alterlab-plotly. A quick static statistical heatmap with sensible defaults is seaborn's territory; defers to seaborn rather than building an interactive plotly figure.
